title: Waypoint
description: Essential Waypoint CLI commands for deployment and management.

commands.description: Initialize a Waypoint project in the current directory.
commands.scenario: Use when setting up a new project to deploy with Waypoint.

commands.description: Build, deploy, and release an application.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to deploy the latest version of your application.

commands.description: View logs for a deployed application.
commands.scenario: Use to troubleshoot or monitor application logs after deployment.

commands.description: Execute a command in a running application instance.
commands.scenario: Use to run commands in the context of your deployed application.

commands.description: Destroy all resources associated with a Waypoint deployment.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to clean up resources after testing or decommissioning an app.

commands.description: Get the current status of a Waypoint deployment.
commands.scenario: Use to check the health and status of your deployed application.

commands.description: Display the current version of Waypoint CLI.
commands.scenario: Use to verify the installed version of Waypoint CLI.

commands.description: Set configuration variables for a Waypoint application.
commands.scenario: Use to update configuration settings for your application.

commands.description: Get configuration variables for a Waypoint application.
commands.scenario: Use to retrieve current configuration settings for your application.

commands.description: Deploy an application without building or releasing.
commands.scenario: Use when you only need to deploy changes without a new build.
Use to release an application version that has already been built.

commands.description: Run a Waypoint server locally for development.
commands.scenario: Use to start a local Waypoint server for testing and development.
